scope: changed-files mode — four orchestrator-supplied paths only: commands/commit.md, hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py, hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py. Other dirty worktree files were not audited.

violations.finding: Contains Chinese stderr diagnostic string: 必须使用正常的UI流程创建session，永远不允许使用代码创建session！ — this is an echo BLOCKED stderr message (user-visible diagnostic string), not a verbatim user-binding quote in docs/dev/ticket-*.md. Standard 6 exemption rules require diagnostic strings to be English with task-id attribution. The line has no English-language comment with task attribution directly above it. The nearby block comment at line ~234 cites docs/dev/ticket-20260509-113838.md (R11 supersession) but that attribution covers a different block. This violation is pre-existing: line 1277 falls outside all diff hunks for this cycle (hunks at new-file lines 1041, 1083, 1103, 1314). Non-blocking under AC-2.6(b): finding is present in the pre-diff baseline and the line is outside the cycle's changed-line ranges.
violations.recommendation: Translate the stderr message to English and add a task-id attribution comment above the block, e.g.: # User-binding directive: verbatim Chinese text preserved at docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md (Standard 6 exemption); echo 'BLOCKED: Must use the normal UI flow to create a session — using code to create sessions is NEVER allowed.' Alternatively, add the verbatim Chinese text to docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md and replace the echo with an English string plus task-id attribution.

standards_passed: no-inline-code (Standard 1): commit.md code blocks are instruction templates passed to the changelog-analyst subagent, not inline implementation logic

no-hardcoded-values (Standard 2): bash_context_strip.py uses environment variable CLAUDE_HOOK_CONTEXT_MAX_CHARS with default 262144; test file HOOK path is computed relative to __file__; BLOCK=2 and ALLOW=0 are exit-code protocol constants, not configurable values

use-source-venv (Standard 3): pretool-bash-safety.sh line 12 sets PYTHON_BIN fallback to python3 only when venv binary is not executable — not a bare invocation; commit.md code blocks all use 'source venv/bin/activate && python3'; test file line 17 is a documentation comment only
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4): no decimal or lettered step numbering found in any changed file
meaningful-naming (Standard 5): no forbidden naming patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, improved-*, better-*, new-*) found

english-only (Standard 6): no CJK text in commands/ directory; agents/style-inspector.md:333 CJK text is exempt per the Cycle-2 mascot rank label exemption (spec-20260518-225715 §5.1); the pretool-bash-safety.sh:1277 finding is pre-existing and non-blocking per AC-2.6(b)

merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7): test_bash_safety_context_rules.py is an E2E integration test driving the real hook process; test_bash_safety_context.py is a unit test of the strip function in isolation — different test levels with different purposes, not duplicates
concise-documentation (Standard 8): commit.md is a dense technical specification with no marketing language, no story-style text, no repetitive examples, and no pseudocode
parameterized-scripts (Standard 9): bash_context_strip.py reads all configuration from environment variables; test file uses derived paths and protocol constants

dangling-references (Standard 10): all scripts referenced in commit.md exist — resolve-close-report.sh, write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py, write-commit-grant.py, step7-spec-update.py all present in scripts/; agents/changelog-analyst.md exists; all Python stdlib imports (json, os, subprocess, re, pathlib) resolve
merge-conflicts (Standard 11): no unresolved merge conflict markers in any changed file

summary.notes: The 1 violation found (Standard 6, line 1277) is pre-existing in the baseline and outside the diff changed-line ranges for this cycle. It is severity=critical by inspector classification but close_blocking=false under AC-2.6(b). Codex consultation confirmed: 0 false positives, 0 missed violations, 8/8 E2E tests in test_bash_safety_context_rules.py pass.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex verified all four changed files, confirmed no false positives on Finding 2 (mascot rank exemption correct), confirmed no missed critical violations. Provided three wording/classification improvements: (1) clarify scope is limited to four supplied paths; (2) rephrase Finding 1 as 'inside changed file but outside changed-line range' rather than 'outside changed-files scope'; (3) report Finding 1 as severity=critical with close_blocking=false rather than calling it 'advisory'. Also ran the new test suite and confirmed 8 passed.

findings.issue: Scope wording is ambiguous — 'changed files in dot-claude repo' could imply full dirty tree was audited, but only four files were examined.
findings.proposed_fix: Add: 'Scope limited to the four orchestrator-supplied paths; other dirty worktree files were not audited.'
findings.rationale: Applied: scope field added to report root to make the four-file boundary explicit.

findings.issue: Finding 1 described as 'outside changed-files scope' but it is actually inside an in-scope changed file — just outside the changed-line/diff-hunk range.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'Pre-existing Standard 6 violation in an in-scope changed file, but outside this cycle's changed-line ranges; non-blocking under AC-2.6(b).'
findings.rationale: Applied: finding location description now correctly distinguishes 'in-scope file, pre-existing line' from 'out-of-scope file'.

findings.issue: Severity ambiguity for Finding 1 — inspector standard says all violations are critical, but calling it 'advisory' conflates severity with close-blocking status.
findings.proposed_fix: Report as severity: critical, plus introduced_in_diff: false and close_blocking: false, rather than labeling it 'advisory'.
findings.rationale: Applied: violation now carries severity=critical, close_blocking=false, and introduced_in_diff=false as separate fields.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All three PROPOSED_FIX items applied: scope boundary made explicit, Finding 1 wording corrected, severity/blocking distinction separated.
